explanations.2: This loads helper libraries that provide extra functions like 'assert_file_exist' that make testing easier.
explanations.13: This regex pattern is intentionally loose (allows any number of digits) because the stricter pattern wasn't working as expected.
explanations.16: This combines the date, time, and timezone patterns into one big pattern that can match log entries with timestamps like '[2022-04-07T18:00:20+0200]'.
explanations.24: The 'run' keyword executes a command and captures its output and exit status so we can check if it succeeded.
explanations.28: This creates a temporary file containing the expected log format so we can use it as a reference pattern for the assertion on the next line.
explanations.29: This checks that the actual log file contains the expected pattern (date, time, and your message together).
